data.name: This portal is the shared entry point for an OMP instance.
data.value: This portal is the shared entry point for an OMP instance.

data.name: No accessible web app instances are currently registered.
data.value: No accessible web app instances are currently registered.

data.name: The admin area is split between manual core administration and advanced automation. For manual setup, focus on instances, hosts, modules, apps, app instances and artifacts. Template- and deployment-driven rollout lives under Automation.
data.value: The admin area is split between manual core administration and advanced automation. For manual setup, focus on instances, hosts, modules, apps, app instances and artifacts. Template- and deployment-driven rollout lives under Automation.

data.name: These pages are the main workflow when setting up and maintaining an OMP instance without HostAgent automation.
data.value: These pages are the main workflow when setting up and maintaining an OMP instance without HostAgent automation.

data.name: Template topology, deployment assignments and deployment tracking are separated from the main workflow until the HostAgent path is more complete.
data.value: Template topology, deployment assignments and deployment tracking are separated from the main workflow until the HostAgent path is more complete.

data.name: Manage admin permissions and role assignments separately from topology and runtime data.
data.value: Manage admin permissions and role assignments separately from topology and runtime data.

data.name: Neutral reference module for web flows in OMP. The page shows a module overview and links onward to the module's configurations.
data.value: Neutral reference module for web flows in OMP. The page shows a module overview and links onward to the module's configurations.

data.name: Continue to the configuration view to review or update the module's current sample configurations.
data.value: Continue to the configuration view to review or update the module's current sample configurations.

data.name: Configurations for Example Web App Module. These values are used by the example module and can be edited without leaving the module interface.
data.value: Configurations for Example Web App Module. These values are used by the example module and can be edited without leaving the module interface.

data.name: Update comment and JSON content for the selected configuration.
data.value: Update comment and JSON content for the selected configuration.

data.name: Principal is already assigned to role.
data.value: Principal is already assigned to role.

data.name: Set Host base URL to make this route clickable from the Portal.
data.value: Set Host base URL to make this route clickable from the Portal.

data.name: Use these pages when defining desired topology for automated rollout.
data.value: Use these pages when defining desired topology for automated rollout.

data.name: These pages are related to deployment orchestration and future HostAgent-driven execution.
data.value: These pages are related to deployment orchestration and future HostAgent-driven execution.

data.name: App definitions belong to modules and describe what can be deployed. Routes, artifacts, installation paths and runtime policy belong to app instances instead of app definitions.
data.value: App definitions belong to modules and describe what can be deployed. Routes, artifacts, installation paths and runtime policy belong to app instances instead of app definitions.

data.name: Choose how the platform should think about this definition.
data.value: Choose how the platform should think about this definition.

data.name: Delete this app definition? Related artifacts and app instances must be removed first.
data.value: Delete this app definition? Related artifacts and app instances must be removed first.

data.name: Delete is blocked while artifacts, app instances or template rows still reference the app.
data.value: Delete is blocked while artifacts, app instances or template rows still reference the app.

data.name: App instances are the runtime center of OMP. This page handles host placement, artifact choice, routing and verification policy for a concrete app inside an OMP instance.
data.value: App instances are the runtime center of OMP. This page handles host placement, artifact choice, routing and verification policy for a concrete app inside an OMP instance.

data.name: Start by choosing the module instance. Host and app selections are validated against it.
data.value: Start by choosing the module instance. Host and app selections are validated against it.

data.name: The chosen app must belong to the same module definition as the selected module instance.
data.value: The chosen app must belong to the same module definition as the selected module instance.

data.name: If set, the host must belong to the same instance as the selected module instance.
data.value: If set, the host must belong to the same instance as the selected module instance.

data.name: If set, the artifact must belong to the selected app definition.
data.value: If set, the artifact must belong to the selected app definition.

data.name: Use either an absolute URL such as https://www.google.com/ or a host-root-relative path such as app01, /app01, app01/ or /app01/. Relative values are resolved directly from the selected host root without any extra portal prefix.
data.value: Use either an absolute URL such as https://www.google.com/ or a host-root-relative path such as app01, /app01, app01/ or /app01/. Relative values are resolved directly from the selected host root without any extra portal prefix.

data.name: Optional absolute URL when the app is externally reachable.
data.value: Optional absolute URL when the app is externally reachable.

data.name: Physical install location on host, if relevant.
data.value: Physical install location on host, if relevant.

data.name: Friendly install slot or service name.
data.value: Friendly install slot or service name.

data.name: Module-owned config identifier. Leave empty when not used.
data.value: Module-owned config identifier. Leave empty when not used.

data.name: Controls what the platform expects this runtime instance to do.
data.value: Controls what the platform expects this runtime instance to do.

data.name: Optional runtime identity that is expected to report heartbeat.
data.value: Optional runtime identity that is expected to report heartbeat.

data.name: Delete removes the runtime row. Make sure no external system still relies on this app instance key.
data.value: Delete removes the runtime row. Make sure no external system still relies on this app instance key.

data.name: Relative path inside your artifact repository or release output.
data.value: Relative path inside your artifact repository or release output.

data.name: Optional checksum used for later integrity checks.
data.value: Optional checksum used for later integrity checks.

data.name: Delete this artifact? App instances depending on it must be updated first.
data.value: Delete this artifact? App instances depending on it must be updated first.

data.name: Delete is blocked while app instances still point at this artifact.
data.value: Delete is blocked while app instances still point at this artifact.

data.name: Hosts represent real deployment targets inside an OMP instance. For manual setup this is one of the most important core pages.
data.value: Hosts represent real deployment targets inside an OMP instance. For manual setup this is one of the most important core pages.

data.name: Every host belongs to exactly one OMP instance.
data.value: Every host belongs to exactly one OMP instance.

data.name: Logical host identity used by OMP. Keep it short and stable.
data.value: Logical host identity used by OMP. Keep it short and stable.

data.name: Optional absolute base URL for this host. The Portal uses it when an app instance has a relative RoutePath. Enter only protocol, host and optional port.
data.value: Optional absolute base URL for this host. The Portal uses it when an app instance has a relative RoutePath. Enter only protocol, host and optional port.

data.name: Free text for the observed or intended OS version.
data.value: Free text for the observed or intended OS version.

data.name: Delete this host? App instances using it must be moved or removed first.
data.value: Delete this host? App instances using it must be moved or removed first.

data.name: Delete this host? App and worker instances are kept, but their host placement and HostAgent deployment state for this host are removed.
data.value: Delete this host? App and worker instances are kept, but their host placement and HostAgent deployment state for this host are removed.

data.name: Host deletion is blocked when app instances or deployments still reference the host.
data.value: Host deletion is blocked when app instances or deployments still reference the host.

data.name: Use host deletion for stale or incorrect host rows. App and worker instances are detached from this host instead of being deleted.
data.value: Use host deletion for stale or incorrect host rows. App and worker instances are detached from this host instead of being deleted.

data.name: Hosts represent concrete runtime targets within an OMP instance. App instances attach to hosts for actual placement and observation.
data.value: Hosts represent concrete runtime targets within an OMP instance. App instances attach to hosts for actual placement and observation.

data.name: Instances are the top-level OMP environments. Templates are optional for manual administration and mainly belong to future automation.
data.value: Instances are the top-level OMP environments. Templates are optional for manual administration and mainly belong to future automation.

data.name: Stable technical key used in links, config and references.
data.value: Stable technical key used in links, config and references.

data.name: Optional context for operators and maintainers.
data.value: Optional context for operators and maintainers.

data.name: Leave empty for a purely manual instance. Template linkage can be added later.
data.value: Leave empty for a purely manual instance. Template linkage can be added later.

data.name: Delete this instance? This only works when no dependent rows remain.
data.value: Delete this instance? This only works when no dependent rows remain.

data.name: Delete is blocked by SQL if hosts, module instances or other dependent rows still reference this instance.
data.value: Delete is blocked by SQL if hosts, module instances or other dependent rows still reference this instance.

data.name: OMP instances are the top-level environments. Instance templates remain optional and primarily belong to the automation workflow.
data.value: OMP instances are the top-level environments. Instance templates remain optional and primarily belong to the automation workflow.

data.name: Modules are definitions. Keep runtime-specific values out of this page and use module instances or app instances for environment-specific configuration.
data.value: Modules are definitions. Keep runtime-specific values out of this page and use module instances or app instances for environment-specific configuration.

data.name: Stable definition key used by apps and instance templates.
data.value: Stable definition key used by apps and instance templates.

data.name: Pick the closest platform role. This is a definition-level classification.
data.value: Pick the closest platform role. This is a definition-level classification.

data.name: Database schema owned by the module, when applicable.
data.value: Database schema owned by the module, when applicable.

data.name: Delete this module? Related apps and instances must be removed first.
data.value: Delete this module? Related apps and instances must be removed first.

data.name: Deleting a module is only possible when dependent apps, module instances and template rows are already gone.
data.value: Deleting a module is only possible when dependent apps, module instances and template rows are already gone.

data.name: Module definitions are reusable building blocks. They do not describe placement or runtime state.
data.value: Module definitions are reusable building blocks. They do not describe placement or runtime state.

data.name: Module instances bind a module definition to a concrete OMP instance. This is where definitions start becoming operational.
data.value: Module instances bind a module definition to a concrete OMP instance. This is where definitions start becoming operational.

data.name: Choose the module definition this module instance represents.
data.value: Choose the module definition this module instance represents.

data.name: Delete this module instance? Related app instances must be removed first.
data.value: Delete this module instance? Related app instances must be removed first.

data.name: SQL blocks deletion while app instances or template mappings still depend on this row.
data.value: SQL blocks deletion while app instances or template mappings still depend on this row.

data.name: Module instances bind module definitions into a specific OMP instance. This is the operational layer above definitions and below app instances.
data.value: Module instances bind module definitions into a specific OMP instance. This is the operational layer above definitions and below app instances.

data.name: Host templates describe reusable host-level desired state for template-driven rollout.
data.value: Host templates describe reusable host-level desired state for template-driven rollout.

data.name: Instance templates belong to the future automation path. They describe desired topology rather than the manually managed live runtime.
data.value: Instance templates belong to the future automation path. They describe desired topology rather than the manually managed live runtime.

data.name: Manage roles, permissions and principal assignments from Portal instead of editing the database directly.
data.value: Manage roles, permissions and principal assignments from Portal instead of editing the database directly.

data.name: Named access bundles that can carry multiple permissions and principals.
data.value: Named access bundles that can carry multiple permissions and principals.

data.name: Low-level capabilities granted to apps and portal areas.
data.value: Low-level capabilities granted to apps and portal areas.

data.name: Create roles, update descriptions, assign permissions and attach principals.
data.value: Create roles, update descriptions, assign permissions and attach principals.

data.name: Create permissions once and reuse them across roles and example modules.
data.value: Create permissions once and reuse them across roles and example modules.

data.name: Permissions are low-level capability names. Keep them stable and descriptive so roles remain understandable.
data.value: Permissions are low-level capability names. Keep them stable and descriptive so roles remain understandable.

data.name: No permissions have been created yet.
data.value: No permissions have been created yet.

data.name: Short explanation of who should receive this role.
data.value: Short explanation of who should receive this role.

data.name: Bind users, groups or service identities to the role. Prefer groups where possible.
data.value: Bind users, groups or service identities to the role. Prefer groups where possible.

data.name: Delete this role? Linked role assignments will also be removed.
data.value: Delete this role? Linked role assignments will also be removed.

data.name: Deleting a role removes all principal and permission links for that role.
data.value: Deleting a role removes all principal and permission links for that role.

data.name: Roles bundle permissions and principals. Use roles to keep direct principal management small and predictable.
data.value: Roles bundle permissions and principals. Use roles to keep direct principal management small and predictable.

data.name: No roles have been created yet.
data.value: No roles have been created yet.

data.name: Describe the capability in business terms, not implementation details.
data.value: Describe the capability in business terms, not implementation details.

data.name: Delete this permission? Role links will also be removed.
data.value: Delete this permission? Role links will also be removed.

data.name: Deleting a permission removes it from all roles.
data.value: Deleting a permission removes it from all roles.

data.name: Use a stable key with letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot.
data.value: Use a stable key with letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot.

data.name: The app could not be saved.
data.value: The app could not be saved.

data.name: The app could not be deleted.
data.value: The app could not be deleted.

data.name: An app with the same key already exists in the selected module.
data.value: An app with the same key already exists in the selected module.

data.name: Delete or update dependent rows first.
data.value: Delete or update dependent rows first.

data.name: Public URL must be an absolute URL.
data.value: Public URL must be an absolute URL.

data.name: Selected module instance was not found.
data.value: Selected module instance was not found.

data.name: The selected app does not belong to the selected module instance's module.
data.value: The selected app does not belong to the selected module instance's module.

data.name: The selected host belongs to a different instance than the selected module instance.
data.value: The selected host belongs to a different instance than the selected module instance.

data.name: The selected artifact does not belong to the selected app.
data.value: The selected artifact does not belong to the selected app.

data.name: The app instance could not be saved.
data.value: The app instance could not be saved.

data.name: The app instance could not be deleted.
data.value: The app instance could not be deleted.

data.name: An app instance with the same key already exists in the selected module instance.
data.value: An app instance with the same key already exists in the selected module instance.

data.name: The artifact could not be saved.
data.value: The artifact could not be saved.

data.name: The artifact could not be deleted.
data.value: The artifact could not be deleted.

data.name: Use a stable host key with letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot.
data.value: Use a stable host key with letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot.

data.name: Base URL must be an absolute http or https URL.
data.value: Base URL must be an absolute http or https URL.

data.name: Base URL must not contain query string or fragment.
data.value: Base URL must not contain query string or fragment.

data.name: Base URL should only contain protocol, host and optional port.
data.value: Base URL should only contain protocol, host and optional port.

data.name: A host with the same key already exists in the selected instance.
data.value: A host with the same key already exists in the selected instance.

data.name: The host could not be saved.
data.value: The host could not be saved.

data.name: The host could not be deleted.
data.value: The host could not be deleted.

data.name: The host is still referenced by data that Portal cannot detach automatically.
data.value: The host is still referenced by data that Portal cannot detach automatically.

data.name: A row with the same key already exists.
data.value: A row with the same key already exists.

data.name: The instance could not be saved.
data.value: The instance could not be saved.

data.name: The instance could not be deleted.
data.value: The instance could not be deleted.

data.name: Schema names must start with a letter and then use letters, digits or underscore.
data.value: Schema names must start with a letter and then use letters, digits or underscore.

data.name: A module with the same key already exists.
data.value: A module with the same key already exists.

data.name: The module could not be saved.
data.value: The module could not be saved.

data.name: The module could not be deleted.
data.value: The module could not be deleted.

data.name: A module instance with the same key already exists in the selected instance.
data.value: A module instance with the same key already exists in the selected instance.

data.name: The module instance could not be saved.
data.value: The module instance could not be saved.

data.name: The module instance could not be deleted.
data.value: The module instance could not be deleted.

data.name: Use letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot. Keep permission names stable.
data.value: Use letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot. Keep permission names stable.

data.name: A permission with the same name already exists.
data.value: A permission with the same name already exists.

data.name: The permission could not be saved.
data.value: The permission could not be saved.

data.name: The permission could not be deleted.
data.value: The permission could not be deleted.

data.name: Use letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot. Keep the role name stable.
data.value: Use letters, digits, dash, underscore or dot. Keep the role name stable.

data.name: A role with the same name already exists.
data.value: A role with the same name already exists.

data.name: The role could not be saved.
data.value: The role could not be saved.

data.name: Select a principal type and enter a principal value.
data.value: Select a principal type and enter a principal value.

data.name: The role could not be deleted.
data.value: The role could not be deleted.

data.name: Allowed characters: letters, digits, dot, dash and underscore.
data.value: Allowed characters: letters, digits, dot, dash and underscore.

data.name: Topology detail pages for InstanceTemplateHosts, InstanceTemplateModuleInstances and InstanceTemplateAppInstances.
data.value: Topology detail pages for InstanceTemplateHosts, InstanceTemplateModuleInstances and InstanceTemplateAppInstances.

data.name: Use a stable role name, for example
data.value: Use a stable role name, for example

data.name: Artifacts are deployable outputs of an app definition. Keep the artifact metadata precise so app instances can reference the right build.
data.value: Artifacts are deployable outputs of an app definition. Keep the artifact metadata precise so app instances can reference the right build.

data.name: Use a known package type when possible so later deployment automation can behave predictably.
data.value: Use a known package type when possible so later deployment automation can behave predictably.

data.name: Deployment records track rollout requests and execution status. They are kept outside the main manual topology workflow.
data.value: Deployment records track rollout requests and execution status. They are kept outside the main manual topology workflow.

data.name: Permissions are stable capability names used by roles and apps. Prefer precise names that do not require frequent renaming.
data.value: Permissions are stable capability names used by roles and apps. Prefer precise names that do not require frequent renaming.

data.name: Roles are the main RBAC editing surface. Add permissions and principals here so access remains grouped and auditable.
data.value: Roles are the main security editing surface. Add permissions and principals here so access remains grouped and auditable.

data.name: These permissions are granted whenever the role is resolved for a user or service identity.
data.value: These permissions are granted whenever the role is resolved for a user or service identity.

data.name: These pages are separated from the main admin workflow because they belong to template-driven rollout, deployment planning and future HostAgent automation. For manual setup, start with instances, hosts, modules, apps and artifacts.
data.value: These pages are separated from the main admin workflow because they belong to template-driven rollout, deployment planning and future HostAgent automation. For manual setup, start with instances, hosts, modules, apps and artifacts.

data.name: Assignments connect hosts to deployment planning. This is intentionally separated from the main manual admin workflow.
data.value: Assignments connect hosts to deployment planning. This is intentionally separated from the main manual admin workflow.

data.name: HostDeploymentAssignments.Lede
data.value: Assignments connect hosts to deployment planning. This is intentionally separated from the main manual admin workflow.

data.name: HostDeployments.Lede
data.value: Inspect HostAgent app deployments, artifact provisioning and explicit template rollout requests.

data.name: You have unsaved changes to the role name or description.
data.value: You have unsaved changes to the role name or description.

data.name: You have unsaved role changes. Press OK to discard them and leave this page, or Cancel to stay.
data.value: You have unsaved role changes. Press OK to discard them and leave this page, or Cancel to stay.

data.name: Workers combines app worker definitions with the live worker runtime so you can manage the worker track from one place.
data.value: Workers combines app worker definitions with the live worker runtime so you can manage the worker track from one place.

data.name: Manage your OMP user profile and portal defaults.
data.value: Manage your OMP user profile and portal defaults.

data.name: Display name must be 200 characters or fewer.
data.value: Display name must be 200 characters or fewer.

data.name: Manage OMP users and their authentication links. Users can exist before they are linked to an AD account.
data.value: Manage OMP users and their authentication links. Users can exist before they are linked to an AD account.

data.name: No OMP users have been created yet.
data.value: No OMP users have been created yet.

data.name: Create an OMP user and optionally add a local password login immediately. AD authentication links can still be added from the edit page.
data.value: Create an OMP user and optionally add a local password login immediately. AD authentication links can still be added from the edit page.

data.name: Create an OMP user and optionally add AD or local password authentication immediately.
data.value: Create an OMP user and optionally add AD or local password authentication immediately.

data.name: User created with AD link and local login.
data.value: User created with AD link and local login.

data.name: Create a local password login now by entering a user name and password. Leave these fields empty to create only the OMP user.
data.value: Create a local password login now by entering a user name and password. Leave these fields empty to create only the OMP user.

data.name: Update the OMP user record and link AD identities that Windows sign-in can resolve.
data.value: Update the OMP user record and link AD identities that Windows sign-in can resolve.

data.name: These provider identities are linked to this OMP user. They are shown read-only in this version.
data.value: These provider identities are linked to this OMP user. They are shown read-only in this version.

data.name: These provider identities are linked to this OMP user. Removing all links prevents sign-in until another link is added.
data.value: These provider identities are linked to this OMP user. Removing all links prevents sign-in until another link is added.

data.name: This OMP user has no auth links.
data.value: This OMP user has no auth links.

data.name: There are {0} direct AD-user role assignments that can be moved to this OMP user.
data.value: There are {0} direct AD-user role assignments that can be moved to this OMP user.

data.name: Move AD rights to OMP user
data.value: Move AD rights to OMP user

data.name: No direct AD-user rights are available to move.
data.value: No direct AD-user rights are available to move.

data.name: Moved {0} direct AD-user role assignments to this OMP user. Created {1} OMP-user role assignments.
data.value: Moved {0} direct AD-user role assignments to this OMP user. Created {1} OMP-user role assignments.

data.name: This OMP user has no AD links.
data.value: This OMP user has no AD links.

data.name: The AD rights could not be moved.
data.value: The AD rights could not be moved.

data.name: Use the same AD key format that Windows sign-in resolves, for example DOMAIN\username, name:DOMAIN\username or sid:S-1-5-21-...
data.value: Use the same AD key format that Windows sign-in resolves, for example DOMAIN\username, name:DOMAIN\username or sid:S-1-5-21-...

data.name: Enter an AD provider user key.
data.value: Enter an AD provider user key.

data.name: AD provider user key must be 1000 characters or fewer.
data.value: AD provider user key must be 1000 characters or fewer.

data.name: This AD account is already linked to this user.
data.value: This AD account is already linked to this user.

data.name: This AD account is already linked to user {0}.
data.value: This AD account is already linked to user {0}.

data.name: The AD authentication provider is missing.
data.value: The AD authentication provider is missing.

data.name: The AD account could not be linked.
data.value: The AD account could not be linked.

data.name: You are signed in with Windows/AD, but this sign-in is not linked to an OMP user account yet.
data.value: You are signed in with Windows/AD, but this sign-in is not linked to an OMP user account yet.

data.name: Create an OMP account to save profile settings and link this AD sign-in to your account.
data.value: Create an OMP account to save profile settings and link this AD sign-in to your account.

data.name: The current sign-in is not an AD sign-in that can be linked.
data.value: The current sign-in is not an AD sign-in that can be linked.

data.name: The AD authentication provider is not available.
data.value: The AD authentication provider is not available.

data.name: This AD account is already linked to an OMP user. Sign out and sign in again.
data.value: This AD account is already linked to an OMP user. Sign out and sign in again.

data.name: The OMP user account could not be created.
data.value: The OMP user account could not be created.

data.name: The local password could not be reset.
data.value: The local password could not be reset.

data.name: The auth link could not be removed.
data.value: The auth link could not be removed.

data.name: Add a local password login linked to this OMP user.
data.value: Add a local password login linked to this OMP user.

data.name: User name is already in use.
data.value: User name is already in use.

data.name: The local password authentication provider is missing or disabled.
data.value: The local password authentication provider is missing or disabled.

data.name: The local login could not be added.
data.value: The local login could not be added.

data.name: User name must be 256 characters or fewer.
data.value: User name must be 256 characters or fewer.

data.name: Password must be at least 8 characters.
data.value: Password must be at least 8 characters.

data.name: Password and confirmation password do not match.
data.value: Password and confirmation password do not match.

data.name: Bind OMP users or AD users to the role. AD group assignment is not available yet.
data.value: Bind OMP users or AD users to the role. AD group assignment is not available yet.

data.name: For OMP users, start typing a display name or user ID and choose a suggestion. For AD users, start typing an existing AD user principal or enter DOMAIN\\User.
data.value: For OMP users, start typing a display name or user ID and choose a suggestion. For AD users, start typing an existing AD user principal or enter DOMAIN\\User.

data.name: AD group assignment is not available yet.
data.value: AD group assignment is not available yet.

data.name: Select an OMP user from the suggestions or enter a valid user ID.
data.value: Select an OMP user from the suggestions or enter a valid user ID.

data.name: This AD account is already linked to an OMP user. Assign the role to the OMP user instead.
data.value: This AD account is already linked to an OMP user. Assign the role to the OMP user instead.

data.name: Manage installation-specific values for config settings registered by OMP upgrades. The allowed setting list is controlled by the platform schema and is not edited here.
data.value: Manage installation-specific values for config settings registered by OMP upgrades. The allowed setting list is controlled by the platform schema and is not edited here.

data.name: Definitions are seeded by OMP. Add or remove definitions through SQL upgrades, not this page.
data.value: Definitions are seeded by OMP. Add or remove definitions through SQL upgrades, not this page.

data.name: Higher priority wins within permission or role scoped rows.
data.value: Higher priority wins within permission or role scoped rows.

data.name: Global rows apply to everyone. User scope wins over permission, permission wins over role.
data.value: Global rows apply to everyone. User scope wins over permission, permission wins over role.

data.name: Values are stored as text and may contain scalar values, JSON, XML, or other serialized configuration data.
data.value: Values are stored as text and may contain scalar values, JSON, XML, or other serialized configuration data.

data.name: No config setting values have been configured yet.
data.value: No config setting values have been configured yet.

data.name: A value for the same setting and scope already exists.
data.value: A value for the same setting and scope already exists.

data.name: Portal entries are curated start points for the destinations you can access.
data.value: Portal entries are curated start points for the destinations you can access.

data.name: Create global Portal Entries that appear on the Portal start page for users with access to the target.
data.value: Create global Portal Entries that appear on the Portal start page for users with access to the target.

data.name: Use a local path such as /content/ex2, a relative path such as content/ex2, or an absolute http/https URL.
data.value: Use a local path such as /content/ex2, a relative path such as content/ex2, or an absolute http/https URL.

data.name: Optional stable navigation entry key. Leave empty when target URL is enough.
data.value: Optional stable navigation entry key. Leave empty when target URL is enough.

data.name: Enter a target URL or target entry key.
data.value: Enter a target URL or target entry key.

data.name: Use an absolute http/https URL, a local path starting with /, or a relative path.
data.value: Use an absolute http/https URL, a local path starting with /, or a relative path.

data.name: Update the Portal Entry shown on the Portal start page.
data.value: Update the Portal Entry shown on the Portal start page.

data.name: This entry is seeded from an app instance. The app target is resolved before custom target fields.
data.value: This entry is seeded from an app instance. The app target is resolved before custom target fields.

data.name: Portal setting definitions are installed by the Portal. This page stores only values that override defaults for this user.
data.value: Portal setting definitions are installed by the Portal. This page stores only values that override defaults for this user.

data.name: Use an integer for int-backed settings. Use text for string-backed settings. Saving the default value removes the user override.
data.value: Use an integer for int-backed settings. Use text for string-backed settings. Saving the default value removes the user override.

data.name: No portal setting values have been configured for this user.
data.value: No portal setting values have been configured for this user.

data.name: This portal setting is not available.
data.value: This portal setting is not available.

data.name: This portal setting uses an unsupported value type.
data.value: This portal setting uses an unsupported value type.

data.name: A value for this portal setting already exists for this user.
data.value: A value for this portal setting already exists for this user.

data.name: Choose which Portal Entries are visible in the topbar and adjust the parent/child layout. Drop a leaf entry on a top-level entry to make it a child.
data.value: Choose which Portal Entries are visible in the topbar and adjust the parent/child layout. Drop a leaf entry on a top-level entry to make it a child.

data.name: Layout parent entry was not found.
data.value: Layout parent entry was not found.

data.name: A Portal Entry cannot be its own parent.
data.value: A Portal Entry cannot be its own parent.

data.name: The layout update would create a Portal Entry cycle.
data.value: The layout update would create a Portal Entry cycle.

data.name: Leave empty for a top-level Portal Entry. Choose a parent to create a child link in the top bar.
data.value: Leave empty for a top-level Portal Entry. Choose a parent to create a child link in the top bar.

data.name: The selected parent would create a Portal Entry cycle.
data.value: The selected parent would create a Portal Entry cycle.

data.name: Instance templates describe what should exist in an OMP instance. Change desired apps here, then let HostAgent materialize the runtime rows.
data.value: Instance templates describe what should exist in an OMP instance. Change desired apps here, then let HostAgent materialize the runtime rows.

data.name: This template is the desired state for OMP instances that use it. HostAgent materializes these rows into concrete hosts, module instances and app instances.
data.value: This template is the desired state for OMP instances that use it. HostAgent materializes these rows into concrete hosts, module instances and app instances.

data.name: Template hosts define the host keys that should exist inside each OMP instance using this template. Concrete hosts report in with the same host key.
data.value: Template hosts define the host keys that should exist inside each OMP instance using this template. Concrete hosts report in with the same host key.

data.name: Module instances are materialized before desired app rows, so apps have a stable module slot to attach to.
data.value: Module instances are materialized before desired app rows, so apps have a stable module slot to attach to.

data.name: Change versions, placement and desired runtime policy here. HostAgent updates the concrete app instances after its next cycle.
data.value: Change versions, placement and desired runtime policy here. HostAgent updates the concrete app instances after its next cycle.

data.name: This row is materialized by HostAgent from an instance template. Change the desired template app instead; this page shows the current runtime result.
data.value: This row is materialized by HostAgent from an instance template. Change the desired template app instead; this page shows the current runtime result.

data.name: Template-managed app instances are runtime results. Change desired versions and placement on the instance template; use this page only for manual fallback rows.
data.value: Template-managed app instances are runtime results. Change desired versions and placement on the instance template; use this page only for manual fallback rows.

data.name: App instances show the materialized runtime state. For template-managed apps, change host placement, version and desired state on the instance template instead.
data.value: App instances show the materialized runtime state. For template-managed apps, change host placement, version and desired state on the instance template instead.

data.name: Hosts are the concrete machines or runtime targets inside an OMP instance. Host templates and assignments describe what each host role should run.
data.value: Hosts are the concrete machines or runtime targets inside an OMP instance. Host templates and assignments describe what each host role should run.

data.name: Host templates describe reusable host roles. Assign a host template to a concrete host so HostAgent knows which desired topology applies there.
data.value: Host templates describe reusable host roles. Assign a host template to a concrete host so HostAgent knows which desired topology applies there.

data.name: Template pages now live under System and Hosts so the admin flow follows the actual model: define desired topology, let HostAgent materialize runtime rows, then inspect the result.
data.value: Template pages now live under System and Hosts so the admin flow follows the actual model: define desired topology, let HostAgent materialize runtime rows, then inspect the result.

data.name: Use these direct links if you reached the old automation page from a bookmark.
data.value: Use these direct links if you reached the old automation page from a bookmark.

data.name: Template hosts define the host keys that HostAgent materializes for instances using this template.
data.value: Template hosts define the host keys that HostAgent materializes for instances using this template.

data.name: Remove this template host? Desired app rows using it must be moved or removed first.
data.value: Remove this template host? Desired app rows using it must be moved or removed first.

data.name: Removing a template host does not delete existing runtime hosts. Move or remove desired app rows that are pinned to this host first.
data.value: Removing a template host does not delete existing runtime hosts. Move or remove desired app rows that are pinned to this host first.

data.name: A template host with the same key already exists in this template.
data.value: A template host with the same key already exists in this template.

data.name: Move or delete desired app rows that use this template host first.
data.value: Move or delete desired app rows that use this template host first.

data.name: The template host could not be saved.
data.value: The template host could not be saved.

data.name: Template module instances are desired module slots. Desired app rows attach to them before HostAgent creates runtime module instances.
data.value: Template module instances are desired module slots. Desired app rows attach to them before HostAgent creates runtime module instances.

data.name: Remove this template module instance? Desired app rows using it must be removed first.
data.value: Remove this template module instance? Desired app rows using it must be removed first.

data.name: Removing a template module instance stops future materialization from this template. Existing runtime rows are not removed by this action.
data.value: Removing a template module instance stops future materialization from this template. Existing runtime rows are not removed by this action.

data.name: A template module instance with the same key already exists in this template.
data.value: A template module instance with the same key already exists in this template.

data.name: Delete desired app rows that use this template module instance first.
data.value: Delete desired app rows that use this template module instance first.

data.name: The template module instance could not be saved.
data.value: The template module instance could not be saved.

data.name: These rows show the latest HostAgent result for each host and app instance. This is usually the most useful view after a desired version has been selected.
data.value: These rows show the latest HostAgent result for each host and app instance. This is usually the most useful view after a desired version has been selected.

data.name: No app deployment state has been recorded yet.
data.value: No app deployment state has been recorded yet.

data.name: These rows show whether HostAgent has downloaded and verified artifact packages on each host.
data.value: These rows show whether HostAgent has downloaded and verified artifact packages on each host.

data.name: No artifact provisioning state has been recorded yet.
data.value: No artifact provisioning state has been recorded yet.

data.name: These rows are explicit template materialization requests. Normal HostAgent app upgrades are shown above even when this list is empty.
data.value: These rows are explicit template materialization requests. Normal HostAgent app upgrades are shown above even when this list is empty.

data.name: Delete this runtime row? If the instance template still expects it, HostAgent may recreate it.
data.value: Delete this runtime row? If the instance template still expects it, HostAgent may recreate it.

data.name: The runtime row could not be deleted. Delete or update dependent rows first.
data.value: The runtime row could not be deleted. Delete or update dependent rows first.

data.name: This worker runtime row is managed by an instance template. Remove or disable the desired template app instead and let HostAgent materialize the change.
data.value: This worker runtime row is managed by an instance template. Remove or disable the desired template app instead and let HostAgent materialize the change.

data.name: Stop the worker runtime before deleting the runtime row.
data.value: Stop the worker runtime before deleting the runtime row.

data.name: The worker runtime row was not found.
data.value: The worker runtime row was not found.

data.name: The worker runtime row could not be deleted. Delete or update dependent rows first.
data.value: The worker runtime row could not be deleted. Delete or update dependent rows first.

data.name: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, Hosts for concrete machines, Apps for deployable components and System for configuration and access.
data.value: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, Hosts for concrete machines, Apps for deployable components and System for configuration and access.

data.name: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, System for artifacts and deployment status, and Administration for configuration and access.
data.value: The admin area is organized around one OMP installation. Use Installation for desired topology, System for artifacts and deployment status, and Administration for configuration and access.

data.name: These pages are the main workflow when setting up and maintaining the OMP installation.
data.value: These pages are the main workflow when setting up and maintaining the OMP installation.

data.name: HostAgent follows the desired installation state and records deployment attempts for review.
data.value: HostAgent follows the desired installation state and records deployment attempts for review.

data.name: HostAgent follows the desired installation state, deploys artifact versions and records deployment attempts for review.
data.value: HostAgent follows the desired installation state, deploys artifact versions and records deployment attempts for review.

data.name: Automation pages now live under System so the admin flow follows the current model: one installation, concrete hosts, deployable apps and HostAgent status.
data.value: Automation pages now live under System so the admin flow follows the current model: one installation, concrete hosts, deployable apps and HostAgent status.

data.name: Hosts are the concrete machines or runtime targets inside the OMP installation. HostAgent reports and deploys apps against these host keys.
data.value: Hosts are the concrete machines or runtime targets inside the OMP installation. HostAgent reports and deploys apps against these host keys.

data.name: Remove this desired app from the template?
data.value: Remove this desired app from the template?

data.name: The desired app could not be removed. Delete or update dependent rows first.
data.value: The desired app could not be removed. Delete or update dependent rows first.

data.name: This app instance is managed by an instance template. Remove or disable the desired template app instead and let HostAgent materialize the change.
data.value: This app instance is managed by an instance template. Remove or disable the desired template app instead and let HostAgent materialize the change.

data.name: Optional files that HostAgent writes after deploying this artifact. Use this for site-specific runtime configuration that should not be packaged in the artifact zip.
data.value: Optional files that HostAgent writes after deploying this artifact. Use this for site-specific runtime configuration that should not be packaged in the artifact zip.

data.name: No configuration files are defined for this artifact.
data.value: No configuration files are defined for this artifact.

data.name: HostAgent writes enabled configuration files after deploying the artifact so site-specific settings can change without rebuilding the zip package.
data.value: HostAgent writes enabled configuration files after deploying the artifact so site-specific settings can change without rebuilding the zip package.

data.name: Path relative to the deployed app root, for example odv.site.config.js.
data.value: Path relative to the deployed app root, for example odv.site.config.js.

data.name: HostAgent writes this text as UTF-8 without BOM and overwrites the file when the deployed copy differs.
data.value: HostAgent writes this text as UTF-8 without BOM and overwrites the file when the deployed copy differs.

data.name: Upload a text file to replace the stored configuration content without posting the file body as a textarea field.
data.value: Upload a text file to replace the stored configuration content without posting the file body as a textarea field.

data.name: Upload a text file or a zip file containing exactly one text file. Zip upload is useful when request filters block script-like text content.
data.value: Upload a text file or a zip file containing exactly one text file. Zip upload is useful when request filters block script-like text content.

data.name: HostAgent writes uploaded text as UTF-8 without BOM and overwrites the deployed file when the deployed copy differs.
data.value: HostAgent writes uploaded text as UTF-8 without BOM and overwrites the deployed file when the deployed copy differs.

data.name: Use this only when the environment allows posting this content as a form field. File upload is safer behind strict request filters.
data.value: Use this only when the environment allows posting this content as a form field. File upload is safer behind strict request filters.

data.name: Delete this artifact configuration file? HostAgent will stop managing the file, but existing deployed files are not removed.
data.value: Delete this artifact configuration file? HostAgent will stop managing the file, but existing deployed files are not removed.

data.name: Deleting this row does not delete files that have already been written on hosts.
data.value: Deleting this row does not delete files that have already been written on hosts.

data.name: Artifact configuration file was not found.
data.value: Artifact configuration file was not found.

data.name: The uploaded configuration file is too large. The maximum size is 1048576 bytes.
data.value: The uploaded configuration file is too large. The maximum size is 1048576 bytes.

data.name: The uploaded configuration file must be valid UTF-8 text.
data.value: The uploaded configuration file must be valid UTF-8 text.

data.name: The uploaded zip file must contain exactly one UTF-8 text file.
data.value: The uploaded zip file must contain exactly one UTF-8 text file.

data.name: Relative path must stay below the deployed app root and must not contain rooted paths or parent directory segments.
data.value: Relative path must stay below the deployed app root and must not contain rooted paths or parent directory segments.

data.name: This artifact already has a configuration file with the same relative path.
data.value: This artifact already has a configuration file with the same relative path.

data.name: The artifact configuration file could not be saved.
data.value: The artifact configuration file could not be saved.

data.name: The artifact configuration file could not be deleted.
data.value: The artifact configuration file could not be deleted.

data.name: An artifact with identical extracted content already exists: {0} {1} ({2}).
data.value: An artifact with identical extracted content already exists: {0} {1} ({2}).

data.name: An artifact with the same app, package type, target, version, and content already exists. No upload is needed.
data.value: An artifact with the same app, package type, target, version, and content already exists. No upload is needed.

data.name: An artifact with the same app, package type, target, and version already exists, but the uploaded content is different. Confirm replacement to overwrite the existing artifact content.
data.value: An artifact with the same app, package type, target, and version already exists, but the uploaded content is different. Confirm replacement to overwrite the existing artifact content.

data.name: Existing artifact content replaced and metadata updated.
data.value: Existing artifact content replaced and metadata updated.

data.name: Existing artifact configuration file rows were preserved.
data.value: Existing artifact configuration file rows were preserved.

data.name: Replace existing artifact with same identity
data.value: Replace existing artifact with same identity

data.name: Only use this when the same app, package type, target, and version already exists and the uploaded content should intentionally replace it. Automatic import never performs this replacement.
data.value: Only use this when the same app, package type, target, and version already exists and the uploaded content should intentionally replace it. Automatic import never performs this replacement.

data.name: The filename references module '{0}' and app '{1}', but that enabled app definition was not found. Register the module/app first or select an existing app.
data.value: The filename references module '{0}' and app '{1}', but that enabled app definition was not found. Register the module/app first or select an existing app.

data.name: Do not include runtime configuration files such as appsettings*.json or odv.site.config.js in the artifact zip. HostAgent writes those from deployment configuration or artifact configuration file rows.
data.value: Do not include runtime configuration files such as appsettings*.json or odv.site.config.js in the artifact zip. HostAgent writes those from deployment configuration or artifact configuration file rows.

data.name: Copy configuration files from previous version
data.value: Copy configuration files from previous version

data.name: Copies artifact configuration file rows from the latest previous artifact with the same app, package type, and target.
data.value: Copies artifact configuration file rows from the latest previous artifact with the same app, package type, and target.

data.name: No previous artifact configuration files were found to copy.
data.value: No previous artifact configuration files were found to copy.

data.name: Copied {0} configuration file(s) from artifact version {1}.
data.value: Copied {0} configuration file(s) from artifact version {1}.

data.name: Configuration files could not be copied automatically. Add them from the artifact edit page.
data.value: Configuration files could not be copied automatically. Add them from the artifact edit page.

data.name: Legacy artifact zips must not include runtime configuration files such as appsettings*.json or odv.site.config.js. Use an OMP artifact package manifest when configuration files should be registered together with the artifact.
data.value: Legacy artifact zips must not include runtime configuration files such as appsettings*.json or odv.site.config.js. Use an OMP artifact package manifest when configuration files should be registered together with the artifact.

data.name: Copies artifact configuration file rows from the latest previous artifact with the same app, package type, and target when the uploaded package does not declare configuration files.
data.value: Copies artifact configuration file rows from the latest previous artifact with the same app, package type, and target when the uploaded package does not declare configuration files.

data.name: Configuration files from the artifact package could not be saved. Review the artifact edit page before deploying this version.
data.value: Configuration files from the artifact package could not be saved. Review the artifact edit page before deploying this version.

data.name: Registered {0} configuration file(s) from the artifact package.
data.value: Registered {0} configuration file(s) from the artifact package.

data.name: Choose a host for runtime apps. Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.
data.value: Choose a host for runtime apps. Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.

data.name: Choose a template host for runtime apps. Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.
data.value: Choose a template host for runtime apps. Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer.

data.name: Only one active desired app row can exist for the selected app and host placement.
data.value: Only one active desired app row can exist for the selected app and host placement.

data.name: An app instance with the same key or active host placement already exists.
data.value: An app instance with the same key or active host placement already exists.

data.name: A desired app with the same key or active host placement already exists.
data.value: A desired app with the same key or active host placement already exists.

data.name: Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer. Runtime apps and non-load-balanced web apps should choose one host.
data.value: Leave host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer. Runtime apps and non-load-balanced web apps should choose one host.

data.name: Leave template host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer. Runtime apps and non-load-balanced web apps should choose one template host.
data.value: Leave template host empty only for host-neutral web apps behind a load balancer. Runtime apps and non-load-balanced web apps should choose one template host.

data.name: When this is turned off, menus open only by click or keyboard shortcut.
data.value: When this is turned off, menus open only by click or keyboard shortcut.

data.name: Use this editor to create or update portable module-definition JSON files. SQL scripts are shown as clear text here and exported as base64 content in the JSON file.
data.value: Use this editor to create or update portable module-definition JSON files. SQL scripts are shown as clear text here and exported as base64 content in the JSON file.

data.name: Use this editor to create manifest-based artifact packages with a deployable payload and deployment-owned configuration files.
data.value: Use this editor to create manifest-based artifact packages with a deployable payload and deployment-owned configuration files.
